item.title: word_count
item.description: Vault Stats This vault currently contains 9,805 words across all notes.

item.title: Unlinked_Pages
item.description: Unlinked Notes Straight ankle lock Buggy choke Close guard overhook stiff Two grip choke on turtle Crusifix Baseball choke Collar grip break downward Cross collar grip break to takedown Grip break on upper back Neck related Side shimmy practice S grip Leg drag Knee slice Double ankle grab pass Torea...

item.title: Empty_Pages
item.description: Empty Notes Canto choke Gogoplata Kimura Figure-four grip North south Rubber guard Double leg .

item.title: Recently_Updated
item.description: Recently Updated Notes index Lockdown Sweep Sweep Collar & Sleeve to takedown Fireman throw Ankle pick Arm drag Single leg Double leg README .

item.title: BJJ journey
item.description: Author I am a 34 year old white belt, who started 08/2023.

item.title: Formatting
item.description: Page title Segment Subsection Info title Body Alert title Body .

item.title: Shin to shin
item.description: General Start off from combat base and a good entry for X guards Steps Opponent has left leg out from and you are on combat base Your “outer” leg’s ankle in fron of their ankle Inner leg wrapped around back of the ankle Hug Kneecap and prepare to fall towards “middle” Don’t fall backwards as opponen...

item.title: Combat base
item.description: General Combat base can be split into three distinct variations: Variants Combat base high Sitting posture which can be used to maintain distance with your opponent when not wanting to fight Standing.

item.title: X guards
item.description: Description Quite vague concept having the legs form a “X” shape while controlling the opponents leg(s).

item.title: Pressure
item.description: When in Knee on belly, Turtle, Mount your need to remember to apply constant pressure to prevent opponent from moving and giving them ground for free.
